sentence: Ro cielo no ye claro hue.
ipa: ˈɾo ˈθjelo ˈno ˈʝe ˈklaɾo ˈwe
gloss_en: The sky is not clear today.
notes: Post-vocalic definite article 'ro' realised as the weak tap [ɾo] — the diagnostic Tena-valley feature (< ILLU / geminate -LL-) per vazquez_obrador2021; distinción ⟨c⟩ [θ] and Ĕ>ie 'cielo' per nagore_panticosa1986.

sentence: Estos zapatos cuestan cuaranta euros.
ipa: ˈestos θaˈpatos kweˈstan kwaˈɾanta ˈewɾos
gloss_en: These shoes cost forty euros.
notes: ⟨z⟩ [θ] distinción and Ŏ>ue 'cuestan' per nagore_panticosa1986; ⟨s⟩+voiced onset sandhi per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Ra reunión ye maitín a ras tres.
ipa: ˈɾa reˈwnion ˈʝe maˈjtin a ˈɾas ˈtɾes
gloss_en: The meeting is tomorrow morning at three.
notes: Feminine and plural post-vocalic article 'ra','ras' as the weak tap [ɾa],[ɾas] per vazquez_obrador2021; 'maitín' (morning) per nagore_panticosa1986.

sentence: Ro mío chirmán bibe cerca d'a plaza.
ipa: ˈɾo ˈmio tʃiˈɾman ˈbiβe ˈθeɾka ð a ˈplaθa
gloss_en: My brother lives near the square.
notes: Masculine article 'ro' [ɾo] per vazquez_obrador2021; initial ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'chirmán' and distinción [θ] 'cerca','plaza' per nagore_panticosa1986.

sentence: Ves de frente y tuerce en ra primera carrera.
ipa: ˈbes de ˈfɾente ʝ ˈtweɾθe en ˈɾa pɾiˈmeɾa kaˈreɾa
gloss_en: Go straight ahead and turn at the first street.
notes: Post-vocalic 'ra' [ɾa] and F- retained 'frente', Ŏ>ue 'tuerce' [ˈtweɾθe] per nagore_panticosa1986; the tap article per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Ra carne d'aquel puesto yera buena.
ipa: ˈɾa ˈkaɾne ð aˈkel ˈpwesto ˈʝeɾa ˈbwena
gloss_en: The meat from that place was good.
notes: Article 'ra' [ɾa], Ŏ>ue 'puesto','buena' and imperfect 'yera' per nagore_panticosa1986; tap realisation per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Án ye ra clau d'o coche?
ipa: ˈan ˈʝe ˈɾa ˈklaw ð o ˈkotʃe
gloss_en: Where is the car key?
notes: Feminine article 'ra' [ɾa] per vazquez_obrador2021; interrogative 'án', 'clau' (key) and ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'coche' per nagore_panticosa1986.

sentence: Cuántas presonas bienen a cenar?
ipa: ˈkuantas pɾeˈsonas bjeˈnen a θeˈnaɾ
gloss_en: How many people are coming to dinner?
notes: Ĕ>ie 'bienen' and distinción ⟨ce⟩ [θ] 'cenar' per nagore_panticosa1986; count question per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Si yo podese, mercarba aquella casa.
ipa: si ˈʝo poˈðese meˈɾkaɾba aˈkeʎa ˈkasa
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that house.
notes: Aragonese conditional -arba 'mercarba' and 'mercar' per nagore_panticosa1986; the autochthonous tap article system per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Ahiere merqué cinco quilos de cereixas.
ipa: aˈjeɾe meˈɾke ˈθinko ˈkilos de θeˈɾejʃas
gloss_en: Yesterday I bought five kilos of cherries.
notes: Distinción ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'cinco' and ⟨ix⟩ [ʃ] 'cereixas' per nagore_panticosa1986; preterite 'merqué' per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Ros ninos puyan ta Panticosa.
ipa: ˈɾos ˈninos puˈʝan ta pantiˈkosa
gloss_en: The children go up to Panticosa.
notes: Masculine plural post-vocalic article 'ros' [ɾos] per vazquez_obrador2021; Aragonese 'puyar' (to go up) and 'ninos' per nagore_panticosa1986.

sentence: Truca-me cuando plegues ta casa.
ipa: ˈtɾuka me ˈkwando ˈpleɡes ta ˈkasa
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: 'trucar','plegar' [ˈpleɡes] per nagore_panticosa1986; ⟨gu⟩ [ɡ] before front vowel per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Buenos diyas, cómo te ba?
ipa: ˈbwenos diˈʝas ˈkomo te ˈba
gloss_en: Good morning, how are you?
notes: Ŏ>ue 'buenos' and 'diya' (day) per nagore_panticosa1986; greeting register per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Más bale paxaro en man que ciento bolando.
ipa: ˈmas ˈbale paˈʃaɾo en ˈman ke ˈθjento boˈlando
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'paxaro' and distinción ⟨ci⟩ [θ] Ĕ>ie 'ciento' per nagore_panticosa1986; proverb per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Ra muller biella pilla l'agulla amariella.
ipa: ˈɾa muˈʎeɾ ˈbjeʎa ˈpiʎa ˈl ˈaɡʎa amaˈɾjeʎa
gloss_en: The old woman picks up the yellow needle.
notes: Article 'ra' [ɾa] plus palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] preserved 'muller','biella','pilla','agulla' per nagore_panticosa1986; the tap article distinct from the retained lateral per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Ro can corre por ras carreras.
ipa: ˈɾo ˈkan ˈkore poɾ ˈɾas kaˈreɾas
gloss_en: The dog runs along the streets.
notes: Articles 'ro' [ɾo], 'ras' [ɾas] as the weak tap contrasting with the trill ⟨rr⟩ [r] 'corre','carreras' — the minimal tap/trill contrast the Tena article creates per vazquez_obrador2021; 'can' (dog) per nagore_panticosa1986.

sentence: Feito y güeit fan una ueitena.
ipa: ˈfejto ʝ ˈɡweit ˈfan una weiˈtena
gloss_en: Done, and eight make a set of eight.
notes: F- retained 'feito' and -CT- > -it- 'feito','güeit' per nagore_panticosa1986; the Aragonese kernel per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Ra finestra d'a montaña ye ubierta.
ipa: ˈɾa fiˈnestɾa ð a moˈntaɲa ˈʝe uˈβjeɾta
gloss_en: The mountain window is open.
notes: Article 'ra' [ɾa], palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'montaña' and Ŏ>ie 'ubierta' per nagore_panticosa1986; 'finestra' (window) per vazquez_obrador2021.

sentence: Plebe muito sobre ros campos berdes.
ipa: ˈpleβe ˈmwito sobɾe ˈɾos ˈkampos ˈbeɾdes
gloss_en: It rains a lot over the green fields.
notes: Masculine plural article 'ros' [ɾos] per vazquez_obrador2021; 'plebe' (rains), 'muito' (< MULTU) per nagore_panticosa1986.

sentence: No creigo que nieve sobre aquel tozal.
ipa: ˈno ˈkɾejɣo ke ˈnjeβe sobɾe aˈkel toˈθal
gloss_en: I do not think it will snow over that hill.
notes: Ĕ>ie 'nieve' and 'creigo','tozal' (hill) per nagore_panticosa1986; negation frame per vazquez_obrador2021.
